name: Metachrysalis Changemaker Community — Fraser Lowland
tagline: Weaving isolated changemakers into one living network.
whyItMattered: Across the Fraser Lowland, dozens of people quietly carry mutual-aid and regeneration work alone, unseen and unconnected. This pilot tried to find them and stitch them into a community where resources, not just money, could flow between projects.
story: Thaumazo set out to find eight local changemakers, weave them into a Discord and the open-source Community Mycelium platform, and let a sociocratic vote allocate the grants. Reality simplified the plan: only eight people finished their profiles in time, exactly matching the eight grants — so the competitive vote was retired — disclosed candidly — and with it the one zero-sum moment in the process.

The real work was relational. 1:1 onboarding calls, weekly drop-ins, in-person game sessions, a potluck, and a 'mycelial mapping' exercise that treated money as just one of eight kinds of resource. People who'd been working in isolation started recognising each other's work — and helping.

The clearest proof came when participants spotted a synergy and one gifted surplus goods to another, redirecting items bound for landfill to a peer-led community supporting unhoused people, arriving just ahead of an atmospheric storm.

Honest texture runs through it: low turnout, busy leaders hard to schedule, a $2,000 loss when co-funding was briefly held as ETH. The Ethereum layer stayed minimal, and — quietly — the entire Localism Fund grant never moved.

signatureStory.title: Three wagonloads, and a bus that wouldn't start
signatureStory.text: Katie Grant has spent a decade — her time, her money, her house — sourcing, cleaning, folding and distributing free supplies to people in need, mostly alone. Nominated by someone who saw her work, she joined the drop-in calls, where the team quickly recognised a synergy with Fiona York, whose peer-led organisation Ayx runs a minibus for the unhoused in Vancouver's Downtown East Side. Fiona listed what Ayx needed; Katie sourced and delivered three wagonloads. Ethan and Daniel spent a Spring Break day picking up and laundering supplies with her, mapping ways to make the work easier, and sourcing a pro-bono accountant to improve her cost recovery. Even the failures turned generative: on delivery day the Ayx bus wouldn't start — a dead battery — but the three-hour wait became a conversation that opened new avenues for supporting the unhoused. A subgroup of changemakers focused on the Downtown East Side began to form.
signatureStory.quote: My takeaway is that I am not alone in this. There are other people out there also doing work that the world tells us is too idealistic and foolish to pursue.

delivered: Ran a real relationship-first local pilot funding eight changemakers at ~$1,800 USD (~$2,500 CAD) each across the Fraser Lowland
Disbursed six grants on-chain — verified at ~$10,778 USDC to six distinct recipients (two further grants paused or never paid)
Built and used the open-source Community Mycelium platform (fraserlowland.org) for changemaker profiles, project pages, and 8-resource 'mycelial mapping'
Convened the cohort through 1:1 onboarding, ~6 weekly drop-ins, in-person game sessions, a potluck, and per-participant deep-dive calls
Catalysed real cross-project mutual aid, including the externally corroborated Katie Grant ↔ Fiona York / Ayx supply-and-bus linkage
Disclosed its pivots candidly — the bypassed vote, scaled-down outreach, and a $2,000 ETH-volatility loss back-filled by Thaumazo

outcomes.text: Previously fragmented changemakers formed a small living cohort, with cross-project support offers and at least one CBC-corroborated mutual-aid linkage
Participants gained new ways to scope right-sized projects and map the many forms of capital flowing through their work
A reusable open-source platform (Community Mycelium) was advanced and put into real use by a handful of local members
The promised on-chain layer — attestations, facet tokens, on-chain governance, dashboards — and a 'bioregional financing' model

status: Continuing as a community — but the LF grant is still undisbursed and the financial record needs correcting

verdict.line: A sincere, relationship-rich local program with six clean on-chain micro-grants and a real open-source platform, but the entire $11,200 Localism Fund grant is still sitting undisbursed as cUSD on Celo (every grant that landed was paid from co-funding), and the Part 3 financial summary overstates disbursement and does not reconcile.

honesty: The community work is genuine and the team is candid about its shortcomings, but the central financial fact is undisclosed: the full $11,200 Localism Fund grant never moved — it sits as cUSD on Celo while all six grants were paid from co-funding USDC on Ethereum. The report's '$0 remaining' doesn't match what's on-chain, 'disbursed to local projects' is overstated, and the totals don't reconcile. No funds are missing — the grant is simply idle, and the report doesn't say so.

proposed.summary: Find and fund eight local changemakers through a peer-nominated, sociocratically governed bioregional grant round.
points: Recruit changemakers by peer nomination into a Discord and the Community Mycelium platform
Adjudicate 32 participants (four per domain) through in-person Metachrysalis game sessions
Sociocratically elect eight to receive $2,500 micro-grants for one-month projects
Disburse grants on-chain via an L2 Safe multisig with EAS attestations and dashboards

stories.title: The grant that never moved
stories.text: The six grants that reached projects were all paid from co-funding USDC on Ethereum. The entire $11,200 Localism Fund grant arrived as cUSD on Celo in December and sat there untouched — the Celo Safe shows zero outbound transfers. The report's claim of '$0 remaining' is false on-chain.

stories.title: When isolation lifts
stories.text: Many participants came in used to structured programs — clear instructions, timelines, defined roles — and the shift into emergent, participant-led work was a learning curve. What surprised the team was how fast these changemakers, long working alone, came together to build on each other's ideas and support each other's projects.
stories.quote: My takeaway is that I am not alone in this. There are other people out there also doing work that the world tells us is too idealistic and foolish to pursue.

stories.title: Funding planned for two years
stories.text: For Kelly Graham Tick's One Caring Human Society, the micro-grant made it possible to launch a hospice RMT program they had been planning for two years — bringing consistency and dignity to end-of-life care, and a more sustainable footing for both recipients and practitioners.
stories.quote: I felt enormous relief and gratitude when told I was selected to receive financial support, as this made it possible to begin a program we've been planning to start for two years.
stories.quoteAttribution: Kelly Graham Tick, Hospice RMT pilot

dimensionEvidence.evidence: The program's genuine strength: it wove isolated Fraser Lowland changemakers into a small living cohort through drop-ins, in-person game sessions and gatherings. The Katie Grant to Fiona York / Ayx mutual-aid linkage — three wagonloads of sourced supplies for a peer-led cooling bus in the Downtown East Side — is externally corroborated by CBC. Durability is still emerging.

Real economic touches — six ~$1,800 micro-grants to local changemakers, a fiscal-hosting project (Chad Kohalyk), and a pro-bono accountant sourced for a participant. But these are one-off transfers; the 'bioregional financing' ambition remains aspirational, and the self-scored 4.5 sits well above the delivered evidence (independent re-score ~2).

Community Mycelium (fraserlowland.org) is a real, live open-source platform with 8-resource 'mycelial mapping' used by a handful of local members. But the promised on-chain layer — EAS attestations, facet tokens, on-chain governance, public dashboards — did not materialise; the grantee self-rates Ethereum 'Minimal,' contradicting the self-scored 5.

correlation.proposed: Find and fund eight local changemakers with $2,500 micro-grants
correlation.outcome: Eight changemakers were funded on paper at ~$1,800 USD (~$2,500 CAD) each, but only six grants were actually disbursed on-chain; two (Jane Diopko, Cans for a Cause) were never paid.

correlation.proposed: Sociocratically elect eight recipients via a competitive vote
correlation.outcome: Only eight participants completed their profiles, exactly matching the eight grants, so the sociocratic election was bypassed — the democratic allocation mechanism reviewers praised did not run. Disclosed candidly in 1.2.

correlation.proposed: Disburse the Localism Fund grant on-chain to local projects
correlation.outcome: The full $11,200 LF grant remains undisbursed as cUSD on Celo; every grant that reached a project was paid from co-funding USDC on Ethereum. Not disclosed in the report.

correlation.proposed: Deliver EAS attestations, facet tokens, on-chain sociocratic record and public dashboards
correlation.outcome: None of the promised on-chain scaffolding materialised; the attestations field is left blank and Ethereum is self-rated 'Minimal.' Not flagged as a change in 1.2.

correlation.proposed: Build and use the open-source Community Mycelium platform
correlation.outcome: fraserlowland.org is live and in real use by a handful of members for changemaker profiles, project pages and 8-resource mapping.
$10,000 hard-committed and on-chain-isolated (11,390.61 USDC from a Kraken hot wallet, 2025-11-26), but single-donor; ~$2,000 was lost to ETH volatility and back-filled by Thaumazo.

On-chain verification confirms the LF grant still undisbursed on Celo

operatorChecks: ! The $11,200 LF grant is still undisbursed as cUSD on Celo — what is the deployment plan and timeline?
! Please provide a corrected Part 3 financial summary that reconciles with on-chain figures and uses one currency.
! What is the status and timing of the two unpaid grants — Jane Diopko and Cans for a Cause / Katryna Cole?
! Can you evidence the $10,000 Scott Nelson co-funding? The Kraken inbound carries no donor identity on-chain.
Please revisit the Economic (4.5) and Technological (5) self-scores against your report's own 'Minimal' Ethereum description.
Was the promised on-chain layer — attestations, facet tokens, dashboards — dropped? If so it should be reflected as a change.
fundingNote: Of the recorded funding, the entire $11,200 Localism Fund grant never moved — it remains as cUSD on Celo with zero outbound transfers. All six disbursed grants were paid from co-funding USDC on Ethereum, and two of the eight funded projects were never paid.
